have h_main : Locally (ClassDL · 𝓕 P) 𝓕 X P := by
    -- Use the fact that X has locally integrable supremum to show that it is locally of class DL
    have h1 : ∀ t : ι, ∃ u : ι, t ≤ u ∧ ClassDL (fun s => X (u : ι)) 𝓕 P := by
      intro t
      -- We can take u = t because X is adapted and the filtration is right continuous
      refine' ⟨t, le_rfl, _⟩
      -- Show that the constant process X_t is of class DL
      have h2 : Submartingale (fun s : ι => X t) 𝓕 P := by
        -- The constant process is a submartingale
        refine' ⟨fun s => _, _⟩
        · -- Prove that the process is adapted
          have h3 : MeasurableSet[𝓕 s] {x : Ω | X t x} := by
            -- Use the fact that X is adapted and the filtration is right continuous
            have h4 : MeasurableSet[𝓕 s] {x : Ω | X t x} := by
              -- Since X is adapted, X_t is measurable with respect to 𝓕_s if s ≥ t
              have h5 : t ∈ orderBotOrderBot (ι := ι) := by
                exact ⟨by simp [OrderBot.orderBot], by simp [OrderBot.orderBot]⟩
              exact MeasurableSet.cut (hX2 t).measurableWith (h_fds.map_le (le_rfl : t ≤ t))
            exact h4
          exact by simpa using h3
        · -- Prove that the process is a submartingale
          intro s
          simp [hX2]
          <;>
          (try simp_all [h_fds])
          <;>
          (try linarith)
          <;>
          (try simp_all [hX2])
          <;>
          (try linarith)
      -- Use the previous lemma to show that the constant process is of class DL
      have h3 : ClassDL (fun s : ι => X t) 𝓕 P := by
        -- Use the fact that the constant process is a submartingale and the previous lemmas
        have h4 : Submartingale (fun s : ι => X t) 𝓕 P := h2
        have h5 : ∀ ω, RightContinuous (fun s => X t ω) := by
          intro ω
          exact rightContinuous_const
        have h6 : 0 ≤ (fun s : ι => X t) := by
          intro s
          simp
          <;>
          (try simp_all [h_fds])
          <;>
          (try linarith)
          <;>
          (try simp_all [hX2])
          <;>
          (try linarith)
        -- Apply the previous lemma to conclude that the constant process is of class DL
        simpa using MeasureTheory.Submartingale.classDL h4 h5 h6
      -- Conclude that the constant process is of class DL
      simpa using h3
    -- Use the fact that for every t there exists a u ≥ t such that X_u is of class DL to conclude that X is locally of class DL
    refine' ⟨fun t => (h1 t).choose, fun t => by
      -- For every t, we have a u ≥ t such that X_u is of class DL
      have h2 := (h1 t).choose_spec.1
      have h3 := (h1 t).choose_spec.2
      -- Use the right continuity of the filtration to conclude that X is locally of class DL
      exact ⟨h2, h3⟩, _⟩
    -- Prove that the process is locally of class DL
    intro t s hts
    -- Since the process is adapted and the filtration is right continuous, we can use the previous results
    have h4 : Adapted 𝓕 X := hX2
    have h5 : 𝓕.IsRightContinuous := h_fds
    -- Use the fact that X has locally integrable supremum to conclude that it is locally of class DL
    simp_all [ClassDL]
    <;>
    (try simp_all [h_fds])
    <;>
    (try linarith)
    <;>
    (try simp_all [hX2])
    <;>
    (try linarith)
  -- Conclude that X is locally of class DL
  exact h_main

    trivial
  -- Use the fact that `ClassDL` is a stable property with respect to the filtration.
  have hX6 : Locally (ClassDL · 𝓕 P) 𝓕 X P := by
    classical
    -- We use a classical case distinction to find a stopping time.
    by_cases h : ∀ t, HasIntegrableSup (X t) P → ClassDL (X t) 𝓕 P
    · -- Case: All processes have locally integrable supremum and satisfy ClassDL.
      have h₁ : ∀ t, ClassDL (X t) 𝓕 P := by
        intro t
        have h₂ : HasIntegrableSup (X t) P := hX3 local_of_mem (Filter.mem_of_superset (by simp) hX2.adaptable)
        exact h t h₂
      -- Since all processes satisfy ClassDL, we can conclude the result.

      -- This is a contradiction because we assumed h : ∀ t, HasIntegrableSup (X t) P → ClassDL (X t) 𝓕 P

      <;> simp_all
    · -- Case: There exists a process that does not satisfy ClassDL.
      exfalso
      -- This is a contradiction because we assumed h : ∀ t, HasIntegrableSup (X t) P → ClassDL (X t) 𝓕 P.
      push_neg at h
      obtain ⟨s, hs⟩ := h
      have h₂ : HasIntegrableSup (X s) P := hX3 local_of_mem (Filter.mem_of_superset (by simp) hX2.adaptable)
      exact hs h₂
  exact hX6

      have hX7 : Adapted 𝓕 X := hX2
      have hX8 : 𝓕.IsRightContinuous := hX3
      -- Use the fact that X has a locally integrable supremum to show that X is locally of class D
      have hX9 : Locally (ClassD · 𝓕 P) 𝓕 X P := by
        -- Use the properties of X and the filtration to show that X is locally of class D
        apply Locally.of_forall (ClassD.of_three_intersections)
        <;> simp_all [hX6, hX7, hX8]
        <;> aesop
      exact hX9
    -- Use the fact that X is locally of class D to show that X is locally of class DL
    have hX10 : Locally (ClassDL · 𝓕 P) 𝓕 X P := by
      -- Use the properties of X and the filtration to show that X is locally of class DL
      have hX11 : Locally (ClassD · 𝓕 P) 𝓕 X P := hX5
      have hX12 : 𝓕.IsRightContinuous := hX3
      -- Use the properties of X and the filtration to show that X is locally of class DL
      have hX13 : Locally (ClassDL · 𝓕 P) 𝓕 X P := by
        -- Use the properties of X and the filtration to show that X is locally of class DL
        apply Locally.of_forall (fun t ↦ ClassDL.of_classD)
        <;> simp_all [hX11, hX12]
        <;> aesop
      exact hX13
    exact hX10
  exact hX4

have h3 : ∀ s : ι, Tendsto (fun t : ι ↦ 𝓐 𝓕 t) (nhdsWithin s (Set.Ici s)) (nhds (𝓐 𝓕 s)) := by
    intro s
    exact nhdsWithin_le_nhds (hfilm.tendsto_ae s)
  -- Use the given has locally integrable sup condition to get the necessary uniform integrability
  have h4 : ∀ s : ι, ∃ u : ι, s ≤ u ∧ HasIntegrableSup (X) (𝓕 u) P := by
    intro s
    have h5 := hX1 s
    -- Extract the u such that X has integrable sup on 𝓕 u
    rcases h5 with ⟨u, hu₁, hu₂⟩
    exact ⟨u, hu₁, hu₂⟩
  -- Use the uniform integrability to show that X is class D on 𝓕
  have h6 : ∀ s : ι, ∃ u : ι, s ≤ u ∧ ClassD X (𝓕 u) P := by
    intro s
    -- For each s, find u ≥ s such that X has integrable sup on 𝓕 u
    have h7 : ∃ u : ι, s ≤ u ∧ HasIntegrableSup (X) (𝓕 u) P := h4 s
    rcases h7 with ⟨u, hu₁, hu₂⟩
    -- Use the fact that X has integrable sup on 𝓕 u to show it is class D on 𝓕 u
    have h8 : ClassD X (𝓕 u) P := by
      have h9 : IsLocallyFinite 𝓕 := by
        -- Show that 𝓕 is locally finite
        refine' ⟨fun s ↦ s, _⟩
        intro s
        simp [le_rfl]
      -- Use the fact that X has integrable sup on 𝓕 u to show it is class D on 𝓕 u
      have h10 : HasLocallyIntegrableSup X 𝓕 P := hX1
      have h11 : HasIntegrableSup X (𝓕 u) P := hu₂
      -- Use the fact that X has integrable sup on 𝓕 u to show it is class D on 𝓕 u
      have h12 : ClassD X (𝓕 u) P := by
        -- Prove that X is class D on 𝓕 u
        have h13 : ∀ t : ι, UniformIntegrable (fun ω ↦ X t ω) 1 P := by
          intro t
          have h14 : Integrable (fun ω ↦ ‖X t ω‖) P := by
            -- Show that X t is integrable
            have h15 : Integrable (fun ω ↦ X t ω) P := by
              -- Show that X t is integrable
              exact hX2.integrable t
            exact h15.norm
          -- Use the fact that X t is integrable to show it is uniformly integrable
          have h16 : UniformIntegrable (fun ω ↦ X t ω) 1 P := by
            -- Prove that X t is uniformly integrable
            exact uniformIntegrable_of_integrable h15
          exact h16
        -- Use the fact that X is uniformly integrable to show it is class D
        have h17 : ClassD X (𝓕 u) P := by
          -- Prove that X is class D on 𝓕 u
          exact hX2.uI classD.2 (fun t ↦ h13 t)
        exact h17
      exact h12
    -- Combine the results to get the desired conclusion
    exact ⟨u, hu₁, h8⟩
  -- Combine all the results to show that X is locally class D on 𝓕
  have h9 : Locally (ClassDL · 𝓕 P) 𝓕 X P := by
    -- Use the fact that X is class D on 𝓕 u for each u to show it is locally class D on 𝓕
    have h10 : Locally (ClassD · 𝓕 P) 𝓕 X P := by
      -- Use the fact that X is class D on 𝓕 u for each u to show it is locally class D on 𝓕
      refine' ⟨fun s ↦ {u : ι | s ≤ u}, _⟩
      -- Show that for each s, there exists a u ≥ s such that X is class D on 𝓕 u
      intro s
      have h11 : ∃ u : ι, s ≤ u ∧ ClassD X (𝓕 u) P := h6 s
      rcases h11 with ⟨u, hu₁, hu₂⟩
      -- Show that X is class D on 𝓕 u for some u ≥ s
      refine' ⟨{u : ι | s ≤ u}, _, _⟩
      · -- Show that {u : ι | s ≤ u} is a neighborhood filter
        have h12 : {u : ι | s ≤ u} ∈ nhdsWithin s (Set.Ici s) := by
          -- Show that {u : ι | s ≤ u} is a neighborhood filter
          have h13 : {u : ι | s ≤ u} ∈ nhdsWithin s (Set.Ici s) := by
            -- Show that {u : ι | s ≤ u} is a neighborhood filter
            apply mem_nhdsWithin_of_isOpen
            · -- Show that {u : ι | s ≤ u} is open
              apply isOpen_Ici
            · -- Show that s ∈ {u : ι | s ≤ u}
              simp
          exact h13
        exact h12
      · -- Show that X is class D on 𝓕 u for some u ≥ s
        refine' ⟨u, _⟩
        -- Show that u ∈ {u : ι | s ≤ u}
        have h14 : s ≤ u := hu₁
        -- Show that X is class D on 𝓕 u
        have h15 : ClassD X (𝓕 u) P := hu₂
        -- Combine the results to get the desired conclusion
        exact ⟨by simp [h14], h15⟩
    -- Combine the results to show that X is locally class D on 𝓕
    sorry
  -- Use the fact that X is locally class D on 𝓕 to conclude the proof
  exact h9

have h3 : Locally (ClassDL · 𝓕 P) 𝓕 X P := by
    filter_upwards [IsRightContinuous.eventually_coeff_le] with t ht
    have h4 : Adapted 𝓕 X := hX2
    have h5 : ∀ ω, RightContinuous (X · ω) := fun ω ↦ by
      have h6 := hX1.t ω
      simp_all [RightContinuous]
    have h7 : IsFiniteMeasure P := by sorry
    have h8 : ClassD (X ∘ fun _ ↦ t) 𝓕 P := by sorry
    sorry
  exact h3
